name: Fridge-Clearing Vegetable Frittata
description: The Kitchn’s foolproof frittata ratio (6 eggs : 1/4 cup cream : 1 cup cheese : ~2 cups veg) — a flexible way to use up whatever produce is in the fridge before traveling.
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup heavy cream or whole milk
3/4 cup grated cheese (Parmesan, cheddar, or goat)
recipeInstructions: Heat oven to 400°F. In a 10-inch oven-safe nonstick or cast-iron skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high. Add bell pepper, onion, and a pinch of salt; sauté until slightly softened, about 5 minutes.
Add zucchini and spinach and cook until wilted and any liquid cooks off, about 3 minutes.
Whisk the eggs, cream, half the cheese, salt, and pepper. Pour over the vegetables, scatter scallions and remaining cheese on top, and cook undisturbed 1 minute.
Bake until the eggs are almost set, about 8 minutes, then broil 1–2 minutes until set and golden. Cool 5 minutes; slice and serve with toast.
author: The Kitchn

ai.cliffNotes: A flexible 6-egg frittata that absorbs whatever vegetables need using up, set in the oven for an even, custardy slice.
keyTips: Cook off any watery vegetables (zucchini, spinach) before adding eggs so the frittata doesn’t weep.
Pull it from the oven when the center is just barely set — carryover and a short broil finish it without overcooking.
Use an oven-safe nonstick or well-seasoned cast-iron so it releases cleanly.
